tavily.answer: Peru has seen an influx of Venezuelans displaced by recent conflicts and natural disasters in Venezuela. The humanitarian situation in Venezuela has worsened due to earthquakes in June 2026, causing over 12,000 people to be displaced and requiring urgent medical care and emergency shelter. The international community, including the United Nations and various NGOs, has mobilized to provide aid, but challenges remain due to damaged infrastructure. The Venezuelan diaspora and international organizations are actively involved in relief efforts, focusing on search and rescue, medical care, and providing essential supplies.
